query_normalized: exploring the impact of data imputation methods on causal inference statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) because it explicitly investigates the impact of handling missing data (specifically comparing standard imputation pipelines against a rigorous non-ignorable missing data method) on downstream statistical outcomes like differential abundance and GWAS, which directly addresses the user's interest in how data imputation methods affect causal/statistical inference.
